insight: In base set, P2 natural Conduit opening loses value if you don't spend the green tech on subsequent turns—the power of Conduit comes from P1 being unable to go Conduit first, not from the tech itself being universally good.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of P2 opening choices in base + Feral/Xaetron/Fibroid set where player went Conduit but rarely needed green tech.

insight: Steelsplitter is strictly worse than Tarsier because it does the same damage but costs more (6B vs 4R), and while Steelsplitter can defend, it cannot defend and attack simultaneously, making the extra cost inefficient compared to buying Walls for defense and Tarsiers for attack.
author: mrguy888
context: Extended debate on Steelsplitter valuation and when it is ever correct to buy over Tarsiers.

insight: When evaluating breach targets, prefer killing units that will cause immediate defensive problems (e.g., a Tarsier that forces you to buy soak next turn) over units that provide slow value (e.g., Vivid Drone providing 3 gold next turn), because in late-game breach situations, the timing of one turn is critical.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of optimal breach target selection and the relative value of eliminating attackers vs. economy.

insight: Thorium Dynamo is breach-resistant not because of its HP relative to cost (which is only slightly better than regular Drones), but because it functions at full power even with damage on it—making it correct to defend with defenders rather than leave it undefended, unlike fragile units.
author: mrguy888
context: Analysis of Thorium Dynamo's actual breach resistance and why defending it is strategically sound.

insight: In early-game economy building, buying an Engineer only makes sense if you will immediately use it to build a Drone; if an Engineer sits without enabling a Drone purchase, it is wasted gold and contradicts the purpose of buying Engineers (to accelerate Drone purchases).
author: mrguy888
context: Analysis of new player opening line DE DDE CC and explanation of why Engineer purchases that don't enable immediate Drone builds are inefficient.

insight: Holding a Scorchilla for one turn (not clicking it) costs approximately one attack value in efficiency, so holding Scorchilla is only worthwhile if you gain at least one attack worth of value from doing so.
author: stacko, velizar_
context: Discussion of Scorchilla holding strategy and the cost-benefit analysis of delaying its clicks.

insight: When comparing attack value to defense cost, one point of damage is generally worth slightly more than 2 gold in a vacuum (typical soak units have better than 2 gold/defense ratios), but the actual value depends on what that damage forces your opponent to buy and what that gold enables.
author: mrguy888, megasupp
context: Analysis of Steelsplitter vs Tarsier decision and why damage value is context-dependent.

insight: Centurion should be prioritized over doubling up on cheaper defenders (like buying multiple Rhinos or Walls) because Centurion provides durable scaling that is better than buying many small units in situations where defensive pressure is high.
author: mrguy888
context: Post-game analysis noting that opponent should have bought Centurion instead of multiple Rhinos.

insight: When holding a unit (such as Dynamo or Scorchilla) to be defended next turn, the same decision logic applies as with breach target selection: evaluate whether the held unit enables problematic defense decisions for yourself.
author: shadourow
context: Discussion of holding mechanics and parallels to breach targeting strategy.

insight: In green-red mirror matchups, the player with better large absorbers (like Xaetron or Defense Grid) beats the player with rush attack—buying more efficient defensive units early guarantees long-term advantage over trying to match opponent's attack.
author: masn6811
context: Analysis of how to beat green-red aggression by pivoting to efficient soak rather than matching attacks.

insight: In base + Zemora set, P1 should go fast Conduit while P2 should go natural Conduit; if opponent commits to Zemora, ignoring it and racing with Tarsiers/Walls/Rhinos is viable because Zemora scales poorly against pure base-set defense.
author: deadhour
context: Discussion of opening strategies and counterplay to Zemora-focused decks.

insight: Against Energy Matrix (a prominent soak unit), fast efficient attackers like Hellhound become significantly less valuable because the absorb barrier is difficult to break through quickly, shifting the game toward slower attack that can accumulate past the barrier.
author: deadhour
context: Analysis of Hellhound overvaluation in a set with high-health absorbers.

insight: In most situations, the 1-damage-per-turn advantage from clicking a prompt damage unit (like Scorchilla or Auride Core) is worth slightly less than 2 gold because it arrives half a turn later than the resource generation from spending that gold on an Engineer.
author: jamberine
context: Discussion of opportunity cost when clicking prompt damage-generating units.

insight: Most early-game games (below Master tier) are decided in the first 3 turns by macro decision-making; micro-level unit choices later in the game have minimal impact compared to early economic and defense decisions.
author: masn6811
context: Comment on game-deciding factors and why early decisions matter more than late-game unit selection.

insight: Bloodpact is an exceptionally strong defensive unit and should be prioritized in many situations, especially when competing against defensive arms races where cheaper efficient defense is more important than attack output.
author: mrguy888
context: Post-game analysis of Bloodpact efficiency compared to alternatives.

insight: In Deadeye-heavy sets, the standard P2 opening is DD DD float DBB, followed by buying out Deadeyes and Drones before investing in other units; this establishes board presence early before transitioning to other strategies.
author: lyra1712
context: Description of standard opening sequence in sets with Deadeye Operative.

insight: In sets with temporary high-attack strategies (e.g., Deadeye bursts), spending attack on breakthrough is only efficient if you can sustain follow-up attack; otherwise, it is better to spend that attack to deny opponent absorb or build permanent damage.
author: siepu
context: Discussion of when to gambit for breach vs when to use attack for denying absorb.

insight: When you can establish an uncounterable Arka threat, accepting a Breach trade to get more total attack is often worse than continuing to build Arka threat, especially if opponent has limited follow-up attack to leverage the breach advantage.
author: siepu
context: Analysis of when to prioritize Arka over accepting breach gambits.

insight: Arka Sodara's primary value is not from denying absorb (though that is a benefit on prompt turns), but from generating 4 attack threat at low cost, which is difficult for most opponents to match or neutralize.
author: megasupp
context: Clarification that Arka's value extends beyond absorb denial mechanics.

insight: When opponent can choose to either absorb your attack normally or hold a unit to receive damage, holding a Scorchilla for absorption forces your opponent to choose between wasting defense on it or taking the damage, generating tempo advantage.
author: velizar_
context: Discussion of Scorchilla holding mechanics and situations where it gains value.

insight: Building temporary breach-proof defense (e.g., via Dynamo or similar units) is valid early-game when incoming damage is low, allowing you to pivot back to normal defense later; however, this competes with efficient absorption strategies and is more situational than pure defending.
author: mrguy888
context: Analysis of temporary breach-proof defense strategy and its limitations.

insight: Prismata decision-making is fundamentally about broad situational assessment rather than universal rules; context such as what defensive resources are available, what your opponent is forced to buy, and what immediate threats exist determine unit value more than cost alone.
author: mrguy888
context: Meta-discussion about how to evaluate Steelsplitter vs Tarsier in different game states.
